mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0% signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of instances following an UNRATE drop below 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with a stable low federal funds rate indicates ample liquidity seeking yield, which flows into equities and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains flat, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of observations following an INDPRO decline during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where PAYEMS increases by more than 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside a declining unemployment rate confirms a synchronized recovery in consumer demand and labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations when HOUST increases and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals an economic contraction that reduces corporate profit expectations and triggers equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters following a negative GDPC1 reading.
